mechanism: NSAIDs inhibit platelet function and may cause GI bleeding; combined with warfarin bleeding risk is substantially increased
management: Avoid NSAIDs if possible; use acetaminophen alternative; monitor INR and signs of bleeding

mechanism: NSAIDs increase bleeding risk when combined with anticoagulants
Additive antiplatelet and anticoagulant effects increase bleeding risk
management: Use only when clearly indicated; GI protection and close monitoring

mechanism: Statins may potentiate warfarin effect via CYP competition
management: Monitor INR when starting or changing statin dose

mechanism: No major direct interaction; both used commonly in diabetic patients on anticoagulation
ACE inhibitors rarely affect anticoagulation; watch for hypotension if bleeding occurs
